title: Inventory investment-flow, project-pipeline, and financing-structure data sources for clean energy in low-income countries
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates investment-flow databases (IRENA, Bloomberg NEF, CPI), project-pipeline data, financing-structure assessments, and energy-access gap data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit sub-national data granularity and financing-structure documentation.
inputs: IRENA World Energy Transitions Outlook documentation
Bloomberg NEF clean energy investment data methodology
Climate Policy Initiative global landscape of climate finance reports
Published missing-middle financing assessments for distributed energy
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly sub-national data granularity and financing-structure barrier documentation.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering sub-national granularity, financing-structure documentation, and concessional-finance allocation data.

title: Map clean energy financing gaps at sub-national scale for at least two low-income countries
outcome: A sub-national financing-gap analysis for at least two low-income countries, documenting investment-flow data sources, sub-national data limitations, and the gap between national aggregates and district-level financing access.
inputs: IRENA and Bloomberg NEF investment data for target countries
Energy-access gap data from national surveys
Administrative boundary data for sub-national aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with financing methodology and data-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms investment data extraction, sub-national granularity assessment, and data-gap documentation for each target country.
done_condition: Financing-gap analysis includes methodology, sub-national results, data-limitation documentation, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze financing-structure barriers including missing middle, concessional-finance allocation, and de-risking instrument coverage
outcome: A financing-structure analysis documenting missing-middle barriers, concessional-finance allocation bias, and de-risking instrument coverage, with explicit volume-vs-access distinction and country-context documentation.
inputs: de-risking instrument documentation from development-finance institutions
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with structure methodology and barrier documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms structure-analysis methodology, barrier documentation, and volume-vs-access distinction for each target country.
done_condition: Financing-structure analysis includes methodology, barrier documentation, volume-vs-access distinction, and replication status.

title: Red-team financing-gap overstatement and volume-implies-access risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how aggregate investment statistics could mask sub-national targeting needs, how volume-gap claims could imply that increasing finance alone closes access gaps, and how missing-middle barriers could be underestimated.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every financing-gap claim has sub-national data documentation, volume-vs-access distinction, and missing-middle barrier context. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every financing-gap claim is either blocked, softened to data-limited language with explicit volume-vs-access and missing-middle caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match development-finance and energy-enterprise decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the financing-gap and structure analysis would change any real decision for development-finance institutions, energy-access enterprises, or concessional-finance managers without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for development-finance and enterprise audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that aggregate statistics mask sub-national targeting needs, or that volume-gap claims imply finance alone closes access gaps. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., IFC distributed energy financing officer), named decision (e.g., concessional-finance allocation to last-mile distributed energy), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for claiming finance volume alone closes access gaps), and replicated field-reality review.
